summary: Follow-up schema-loading hygiene noted while decomposing structural complexity budget reporting and adding a bundled schema fallback for that report.

observations.surface: ops/scripts/schema_runtime.py and schema-backed report runtimes that call load_schema(vault / schema_rel_path)
observations.problem: Policy contract validation, the structural complexity budget report, auto-improve readiness, and mutation proposal reporting now use a shared vault-override plus bundled-schema fallback helper, but many other schema-backed runtime reports still load schemas only from the active vault path.
observations.why_it_matters: Temp-vault and packaged-copy workflows can fail because report builders appear self-contained while actually depending on a full repo schema tree beside the input vault.
observations.automation_candidate: Continue using load_schema_with_vault_override from schema_runtime.py to make the vault override versus bundled schema fallback policy explicit, then migrate report generators one artifact family at a time.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-23 KST for the current P1 self-improvement/report tranche: auto_improve_runtime, mechanism_review_runtime, mechanism_review_history_runtime, experiment_telemetry_runtime, mechanism_run_common_runtime, mechanism_run_scaffold_resolution_runtime, planning_gate_artifact_runtime, and proposal_scope_runtime now use the shared vault-override plus bundled-schema fallback helper. Direct vault schema loads dropped from 42 calls across 30 files to 29 calls across 22 files. Keep the remaining supply-chain/report-writer migration as a staged follow-up, preserving the same vault override and bundled fallback semantics.
